steps.title: Find the equation of the boundary line
steps.content: The line crosses the y-axis at (0, -6). Using a second point at (9, 0), the slope is $$\frac{0-(-6)}{9-0} = \frac{6}{9} = \frac{2}{3}$$ Using point-slope form, the boundary line is:

steps.title: Determine the inequality from the shaded region
steps.content: The shaded region lies above the boundary line, so we use $\geq$. The inequality is $$y \geq \frac{2}{3}x - 6$$ That matches choice A — A is correct.
